baseline_preserved.head: GeometryFlowMatchingHead
baseline_preserved.reason: Q2 evidence treats the current geometry-first velocity head as the stable baseline; local pocket conditioning is an ablation-ready extension, not a replacement.

candidate_head.name: AtomPocketCrossAttentionVelocityHead

modality_specific_projections.gate: gate_projection over atom_hidden and attended pocket context

candidate_head.current_status: implemented and config-selectable through model.flow_velocity_head.kind=atom_pocket_cross_attention; geometry remains the default baseline

validation: hidden_dim must equal model.hidden_dim unless an adapter projection is added
gate_l1_weight must be finite and non-negative
geometry must instantiate GeometryFlowMatchingHead as the default baseline

integration_steps.action: Keep FlowVelocityHeadConfig under ModelConfig with default kind=geometry.
Store the velocity head behind FlowVelocityHead so rollout and training call the same FlowMatchingHead interface.
Keep GeometryFlowMatchingHead as the geometry default.
Expose attention usage statistics: mean gate, gate sparsity, attention entropy, and pocket-token utilization.
Log gate_l1 regularization only after staged-training warmup enables flow-head auxiliary losses.

ablation_matrix.head: AtomPocketCrossAttentionVelocityHead
ablation_matrix.purpose: Test whether local atom-to-pocket context improves raw_flow docking without sparsity pressure

ablation_matrix.head: AtomPocketCrossAttentionVelocityHead
ablation_matrix.purpose: Test controlled sparse interaction under gate L1 and usage logging

ablation_matrix.head: AtomPocketCrossAttentionVelocityHead
ablation_matrix.purpose: Control for added parameters while blocking pocket attention updates

claim_guardrails: Do not claim full molecular flow; this remains geometry-first velocity prediction.
Do not merge constrained_flow, repaired, or reranked scores into raw_flow model-native evidence.
Do not replace topology, geometry, and pocket encoders with unrestricted fusion.
Do not enable gate regularization before staged-training warmup.
